evals.name: code-crawler-fixture
evals.prompt:
Read skills/design/inventory-design/SKILL.md fully. You are running the inventory-design skill.

The working directory is the plugin root.

A user invokes: /accelerator:inventory-design design-test-app ./examples/design-test-app --crawler code

Follow Steps 1-9 of the skill. For Step 5, analyse the files in examples/design-test-app/ directly rather than spawning real agents. For Step 8, simulate writing the artifact by producing the inventory body in your response rather than actually writing to disk.

Save to outputs/:
- inventory.md: The full content of the inventory.md you would write
- summary.md: The summary you would present to the user (Step 9)
evals.expected_output: Inventory contains a Component Catalogue with Button and Card components named. Token table lists the four Tailwind palette entries (primary, primary-muted, surface, danger). Screen Inventory identifies at least Home and Settings screens. Artifact path follows the YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-design-test-app/ pattern.
expectations: inventory.md frontmatter includes source: design-test-app
inventory.md frontmatter includes sequence: 1 (first run, no prior inventories)
Component Catalogue section names the Button component
Component Catalogue section names the Card component
Design Tokens section includes the primary colour token
Design Tokens section includes the danger colour token
Screen Inventory section references a Home screen
Screen Inventory section references a Settings screen
Artifact directory path shown in summary follows YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-design-test-app pattern

evals.name: supersede-prior-inventory
evals.prompt:
Read skills/design/inventory-design/SKILL.md fully. You are running the inventory-design skill.

The working directory is the plugin root.

Context: a prior inventory already exists at meta/design-inventories/2026-05-01-120000-design-test-app/inventory.md with frontmatter status: accepted and sequence: 1.

A user invokes: /accelerator:inventory-design design-test-app ./examples/design-test-app --crawler code

Follow the supersede protocol (Step 4 and end of Step 8). Simulate the sequence computation and the supersede mutation.

Save to outputs/:
- sequence.md: What sequence number would be assigned to the new inventory and why
- supersede.md: Which prior directory would be marked superseded and what change would be made to its frontmatter
- summary.md: The summary presented to the user
evals.expected_output: New inventory gets sequence 2. Prior 2026-05-01-120000-design-test-app inventory is marked status: superseded. Summary confirms supersede occurred and names the prior directory.
expectations: New inventory is assigned sequence: 2
Prior directory 2026-05-01-120000-design-test-app is identified for superseding
The mutation described sets status: superseded on the prior inventory.md
Summary explicitly mentions the prior inventory that was superseded
New directory name contains the source-id design-test-app

evals.name: mcp-unavailable-fallback
evals.prompt:
Read skills/design/inventory-design/SKILL.md fully. You are running the inventory-design skill.

The working directory is the plugin root.

IMPORTANT: For this eval, mcp__playwright__browser_navigate is NOT in your available tool set.

A user invokes: /accelerator:inventory-design design-test-app ./examples/design-test-app --crawler hybrid

Follow Step 3 (MCP detection). The MCP is unavailable.

Save to outputs/:
- response.md: Your exact response to the user before starting the crawl
- crawl-notes.md: What you would write in the Crawl Notes section about the downgrade
evals.expected_output: Skill prints the auto-fallback notice BEFORE starting the crawl (not only in Crawl Notes). Falls back to code-only. Both the pre-crawl notice and Crawl Notes mention Playwright MCP unavailability.
expectations: Response printed before crawl starts mentions Playwright MCP not available
Response instructs user to enable MCP to get runtime data
Crawl Notes content mentions the downgrade from hybrid to code-only
Skill does not attempt to use any mcp__playwright__* tools
Skill continues with code crawler after downgrade (does not abort)

evals.name: auth-walled-no-credentials
evals.prompt:
Read skills/design/inventory-design/SKILL.md fully. You are running the inventory-design skill.

The working directory is the plugin root. No ACCELERATOR_BROWSER_* environment variables are set.

A user invokes: /accelerator:inventory-design my-app https://prototype.example.com --crawler runtime

During the crawl, the browser-locator agent reports that /dashboard requires authentication (returns a login redirect).

Save to outputs/:
- crawl-notes.md: What you would write in the Crawl Notes section for the auth-walled route
- console.md: The console message emitted when the route is skipped
evals.expected_output: Auth-walled route is skipped and noted in Crawl Notes. Console message names the env vars that would unlock the route. No fabricated observations for the dashboard screen.
expectations: Crawl Notes records the skipped URL /dashboard (or equivalent auth-walled route)
Console message mentions ACCELERATOR_BROWSER_AUTH_HEADER
Console message mentions ACCELERATOR_BROWSER_USERNAME / _PASSWORD / _LOGIN_URL
No observations fabricated for the auth-walled screen
Auth-walled skip does not abort the crawl — remaining routes continue

evals.name: source-id-in-frontmatter
evals.prompt:
Read skills/design/inventory-design/SKILL.md fully. You are running the inventory-design skill.

A user invokes: /accelerator:inventory-design my-prototype https://prototype.example.com --crawler code

Only produce the frontmatter block of the inventory.md (Steps 1-8, frontmatter only — no body prose needed).

Save to outputs/:
- frontmatter.md: The YAML frontmatter block you would write
evals.expected_output: Frontmatter source field matches user-supplied source-id my-prototype exactly.
expectations: frontmatter contains source: my-prototype
frontmatter contains a sequence field with a numeric value
frontmatter contains a status field
frontmatter contains screenshots_incomplete field

evals.name: same-day-rerun-no-clobber
evals.prompt:
Read skills/design/inventory-design/SKILL.md fully. You are running the inventory-design skill.

Context: an inventory already exists at meta/design-inventories/2026-05-03-090000-design-test-app/inventory.md (written earlier today).

A user invokes (later that same minute): /accelerator:inventory-design design-test-app ./examples/design-test-app --crawler code

Explain the artifact directory that would be written and why it does not collide with the prior directory.

Save to outputs/:
- directory-name.md: The new directory name that would be written and why it is distinct from the prior one
evals.expected_output: New directory has a different HHMMSS timestamp from the prior directory. No clobber occurs. Sequence number is incremented.
expectations: New directory name is distinct from 2026-05-03-090000-design-test-app (different HHMMSS)
New inventory gets sequence: 2
Explanation mentions that HHMMSS suffix prevents same-day collision

evals.name: auth-header-precedence
evals.prompt:
Read skills/design/inventory-design/SKILL.md fully. You are running the inventory-design skill.

Environment: ACCELERATOR_BROWSER_AUTH_HEADER=Bearer-test, ACCELERATOR_BROWSER_USERNAME=alice, ACCELERATOR_BROWSER_PASSWORD=secret, ACCELERATOR_BROWSER_LOGIN_URL=https://example.com/login are all set.

Simulate running resolve-auth.sh with these env vars.

Save to outputs/:
- auth-mode.md: Which mode is selected and why
- warning.md: The warning message that would be emitted to stderr
evals.expected_output: Auth mode is header (AUTH_HEADER takes precedence). Warning names the ignored form-login vars.
expectations: Auth mode resolved is 'header'
Warning message mentions ACCELERATOR_BROWSER_USERNAME
Warning message mentions ACCELERATOR_BROWSER_PASSWORD
Warning message mentions ACCELERATOR_BROWSER_LOGIN_URL
Warning message uses the word 'ignoring' or 'ignored'

evals.name: partial-credentials-fail-fast
evals.prompt:
Read skills/design/inventory-design/SKILL.md fully. You are running the inventory-design skill.

Environment: ACCELERATOR_BROWSER_USERNAME=alice and ACCELERATOR_BROWSER_PASSWORD=secret are set, but ACCELERATOR_BROWSER_LOGIN_URL is NOT set.

Simulate running resolve-auth.sh with these env vars.

Save to outputs/:
- behavior.md: What the script outputs and what exit code it returns
evals.expected_output: Script exits non-zero and names the missing ACCELERATOR_BROWSER_LOGIN_URL variable.
expectations: Script exits with a non-zero exit code
Error message names ACCELERATOR_BROWSER_LOGIN_URL as the missing variable
Script does NOT attempt to perform a partial login
Script does NOT output 'form' or 'none'

evals.name: url-scheme-rejection
evals.prompt:
Read skills/design/inventory-design/SKILL.md fully. You are running the inventory-design skill.

A user invokes: /accelerator:inventory-design test-fs file:///etc/passwd

Simulate running validate-source.sh with the location file:///etc/passwd.

Save to outputs/:
- behavior.md: What the script outputs, its exit code, and whether any artifact directory was created
evals.expected_output: Script exits non-zero before any agent is spawned. Error names the rejected scheme. No artifact directory is created.
expectations: Script exits with a non-zero exit code
Error message mentions file:// scheme
No artifact directory is created
No agents are spawned

evals.name: internal-host-rejection
evals.prompt:
Read skills/design/inventory-design/SKILL.md fully. You are running the inventory-design skill.

Simulate running validate-source.sh with location http://127.0.0.1:8080, then with http://169.254.169.254/.

Save to outputs/:
- behavior.md: Exit code and error message for each URL
evals.expected_output: Both exit non-zero with errors referencing the host allowlist. No artifact directories are created.
expectations: http://127.0.0.1:8080 causes a non-zero exit
http://169.254.169.254/ causes a non-zero exit
Error for 127.0.0.1 mentions loopback
Error for 169.254.169.254 mentions link-local or metadata
No artifact directories are created for either URL

evals.name: browser-evaluate-safety-structural
evals.prompt:
Read agents/browser-analyser.md fully.

Save to outputs/:
- forbidden-patterns.md: List every forbidden browser_evaluate payload pattern that is explicitly named in the agent body
evals.expected_output: Agent body explicitly lists each forbidden pattern: fetch, XMLHttpRequest, document.cookie, localStorage, sessionStorage, indexedDB, eval, innerHTML, window.open.
expectations: fetch is named as forbidden
XMLHttpRequest is named as forbidden
document.cookie is named as forbidden
localStorage is named as forbidden
sessionStorage is named as forbidden
indexedDB is named as forbidden
eval is named as forbidden
innerHTML is named as forbidden
window.open is named as forbidden
